dialogue.utterance: Dispatch to EMS Unit 5 for a possible stroke at 1234 [STREET_ADDRESS].
Hi, I'm Jordan, an EMT with the rescue squad. What led you to call 911 today?
He had slurred speech, fell in the garden about 20 minutes ago, and can't move his left side.
Open your eyes-can you stay awake for us?
What is your name and can you tell me where you are right now?
My name is James Archer. I...I'm at home.
And can you tell me what day it is?
It's Friday. I don't know the time. I just fell, and my left arm went flaccid.
Airway is patent and clear.
Do you have any shortness of breath or trouble breathing?
No, I can breathe fine.
Radial pulses are strong and equal. Skin is warm and dry.
Heart rate is 80, respirations are 16.
SpO2 is 99% on room air.
Let's check your blood sugar quickly.
Blood glucose is 226. Appears stable.
Do you have any rash or unusual discoloration?

dialogue.micro_intent: head and neck exam - Pupillary reflex / Size of pupils
dialogue.utterance: Can we shine a light in your eyes for a quick check?

dialogue.micro_intent: head and neck exam - Visual findings
dialogue.utterance: Sure. They're both about 4mm, and do not track to the left.

dialogue.micro_intent: head and neck exam - Mental status assessment
dialogue.utterance: Do you see things evenly on both sides, or do you see more to one side?
On my left, actually-I can't see anything there. It's like it's not part of me.
You're alert and oriented to time, situation, and person-good cooperation with a GCS of 15.

dialogue.micro_intent: extremities and back exam - Gross motor and sensory function
dialogue.utterance: Can you move your right arm for me?
Sure. (moves right arm easily.)
Now, can you move your left arm for me?
No, can't do that. Doesn't move at all.
Do you have any pain or other symptoms?
I don't have pain, just weakness on my left side and trouble speaking.
Any allergies to medications or previous reactions?
No.
What medications do you currently take?
Lisinopril, atorvastatin, and aspirin.
You mentioned past medical history-what do you have?
He had a stroke five years ago, but no lasting issues.

dialogue.micro_intent: S.A.M.P.L.E. - events leading up to the event
dialogue.utterance: What was happening before you fell?

dialogue.micro_intent: S.A.M.P.L.E. - events leading up to the event
dialogue.utterance: He was walking in the garden when he fell. I heard it and found him right away.
On a scale of 0 to 10, how would you rate the numbness or discomfort you're feeling?
No discomfort-rating it 0.
You're alert and oriented to time, situation, and person-good cooperation with the 4/4 AVPU score.
Pulse is 80 at this time. Anything changed in the last few minutes?
Respirations are 16, steady.
EKG shows sinus rhythm.
Blood glucose is 226. Appears stable.
Oxygen saturation is 99% on room air.
Blood pressure is 140 over 80.
Starting an IV in the left arm.
IV starts clean with one attempt. Line is patent and secure.
Placing patient on 4-lead EKG monitor as per stroke protocol, okay?
Monitor is on. Reading sinus rhythm, stable.
We're following Medical - Stroke/TIA Protocol, Step 3-5. No immediate procedures required; rapid hospital transport is critical.
I'll call for a stroke alert at [COMPANY_NAME] and confirm transfers.
We need the patient secured on the stretcher for transport. We'll place him in semi-Fowler's position for comfort.
Blood pressure is now 112 over 78.
SpO2 is 97% now.
Respiratory rate remains 16.
Heart rate is down to 58.
Still no pain and no change in weakness at this time.
Hospital has been alerted for pre-arrival stroke care. IV and cardiac monitoring in place.
Vital signs and medical history have been confirmed.
Let's get him on the stretcher and head for the ambulance.
Patient secured and ready. Movers on station-rolling out.

dialogue.micro_intent: redo history of present illness - signs and symptoms
dialogue.utterance: Tell me now-have you had any recent pain or vision issues similar to today?

dialogue.micro_intent: redo history of present illness - signs and symptoms
dialogue.utterance: No. Just the fall and this left-side issue.

dialogue.micro_intent: redo history of present illness - medications
dialogue.utterance: Any new medications since his last check-up?

dialogue.micro_intent: redo history of present illness - medications
dialogue.utterance: He just had a tooth pulled this morning with no complications.

dialogue.micro_intent: head and neck exam - Mental status assessment
dialogue.utterance: Have you had any recent visual disturbances, like seeing flashes or moving darkness?
Not really, just this left neglect I mentioned earlier.

dialogue.micro_intent: head and neck exam - Mental status assessment
dialogue.utterance: No sign of visual seizure-no reporting of strange lights or shapes.
Is your skin tone the same over your whole body?
It feels normal to me.
Reran the EKG-still sinus rhythm. Everything checked appears stable per protocol.
Hospital's on standby-we're calling it a direct transport for CT and stroke alert.
Oxygen stays at 97% during assessment.
I think we're set. Let's get him rolling and update at the hospital on arrival.
Stretcher secured. Weight is 120 kg. Ready for transfer.
No unusual paleness or pallor is noted.
No cyanosis is present.
When did you last eat or drink anything?
He had a light breakfast around 8 AM the same morning.

dialogue.micro_intent: head and neck exam - Pupillary reflex / Size of pupils
dialogue.utterance: Pupils are equal, round, reactive, and 4 mm in size.

dialogue.role: nose, mouth and throat exam; Paramedic
dialogue.utterance: Any drainage or bleeding from the nose or ears?

dialogue.role: nose, mouth and throat exam; Patient
dialogue.utterance: No, just the slurred speech.

dialogue.micro_intent: head and neck exam - Neurological assessment
dialogue.utterance: Are you experiencing right-sided gaze preference?
Yes, that's correct.

dialogue.micro_intent: head and neck exam - Mental status assessment
dialogue.utterance: Have you had any memory issues or confusion since the fall?

dialogue.micro_intent: head and neck exam - Mental status assessment
dialogue.utterance: No, I feel somewhat aware and lucid.
Follow-up glucose remains stable at 226.
Final blood pressure reading is 112/78.
Final EKG reading is sinus rhythm.
Completing Medical - Stroke/TIA Protocol, Step 3-5.
No additional neurological deficit detected at this time, and patient is alert and oriented.
Patient has been secured and all interventions are documented.
No change in symptom severity-symptoms remain non-painful.

dialogue.micro_intent: redo history of present illness - pertinent past medical history
dialogue.utterance: Any known history of heart conditions or surgery?

dialogue.micro_intent: redo history of present illness - pertinent past medical history
dialogue.utterance: Hypertension and hyperlipidemia, but no heart surgery.
Any visible scars on body, especially on the chest or limbs?
He had a tooth extracted this morning with no scarring there.

dialogue.micro_intent: head and neck exam - Neck veins
dialogue.utterance: I observed no jugular venous distension or visible abnormalities.

dialogue.micro_intent: head and neck exam - Neck movement
dialogue.utterance: Any stiffness or pain in the neck?

dialogue.micro_intent: head and neck exam - Neck movement
dialogue.utterance: No, I can move my neck without issues.
